task:
Decompose large issue #2577 into smaller sub-issues ## Context The original issue has been flagged as too large for a single implementation: - Estimated lines: 3300 - Estimated files: 15 - Files: packages/mastra/src/mastra.ts, packages/ai-gateway/src/langfuse/prompt-service.ts, packages/ai-gateway/src/index.ts, deck-rewrite.ts, packages/mastra/src/agents/index.ts, packages/ai-gateway/src/prompts/templates/deck-rewrite.ts, packages/mastra/src/resilience/resilient-agent-runner.ts, rewrite-artifacts.ts, editor-agent.ts, packages/mastra/src/rag/context-provider.ts... ## Size Constraints Each sub-issue must respect these guardrail limits: - Maximum files changed: 25 - Maximum additions: 500 lines - Maximum net deletions: 200 lines ## Your Task You need to decompose this large issue into 2-4 smaller sub-issues that can each be implemented independently. ### Step 1: Read the Original Issue ```bash gh issue view 2577 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes ``` ### Step 2: Analyze the CodeRabbit Plan ``` ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Create a dedicated rewriter agent in Mastra with model routing and RAG support, following the established editor/partner agent patterns - Build prompt templates leveraging the existing `outlineRewriteInstructions` partial with audience brief interpolation for tone adaptation - Implement server actions as preview-then-apply flow, reusing `saveStoryboardContent` for persistence - Keep the rewriter as a standalone action rather than automatic workflow step, giving users explicit control over destructive content changes
Design Choices
Design Choice 1: Where should the rewriter run in the workflow? **Options Considered:** 1. Add to `postProcessWorkflow` parallel fan-out alongside partner/validator/whisperer/editor 2. Sequential step after `postProcessWorkflow` merge 3. Standalone server action triggered independently **Chosen Option:** 3 **Rationale:** Standalone server action. The rewriter applies destructive changes to slide content (unlike review agents that suggest), so it should be an explicit user-triggered action rather than automatic in the workflow.
Design Choice 2: Should rewrite output go to a new table or update existing storyboard_contents? **Options Considered:** 1. Create new `rewrite_outputs` table to preserve original 2. Update `storyboard_contents` directly with rewritten slides **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** Update `storyboard_contents` directly. The existing `saveStoryboardContent` service supports upserts, and storing intermediate versions adds complexity without clear value. Users can regenerate if needed.
💡 User Tips Regenerate the plan with different choices with `@coderabbitai `. ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Rewriter Agent Infrastructure Create the Mastra agent with model routing, RAG context support, and output schemas for rewritten content and speaker notes.
Task 1: Create Rewriter Agent Define the rewriter agent following the established agent pattern with structured output schemas. - Create `packages/mastra/src/agents/rewriter-agent.ts` following `editor-agent.ts` pattern - Define `RewriterSlideSchema` with fields: `slideId`, `rewrittenHeadline`, `rewrittenBody`, `speakerNotes`, `rationale` - Define `RewriterOutputSchema` as array of `RewriterSlideSchema` plus `overallSummary` - Agent instructions should emphasize: clarity, impact, presentation best practices, audience tone adaptation - Use `createDynamicModelForAgent("rewriter")` for runtime model swapping
Task 2: Add Model Routing Configuration Wire the rewriter into the model routing system for fallback chains and task-type routing. - Update `packages/mastra/src/config/model-routing.ts`: add `"rewriter"` to `AgentName` union - Add `rewriter` entry to `AGENT_MODEL_POLICY` with default `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514`, fast `openai/gpt-4o-mini`, fallbacks including `openai/gpt-4o` - Update `packages/mastra/src/resilience/resilient-agent-runner.ts`: add `case "rewriter"` to `resolveAgentRunner()` switch
Task 3: Configure RAG Retrieval Enable the rewriter to access user uploads and playbooks for context-aware rewriting. - Update `packages/mastra/src/rag/context-provider.ts`: add `"rewriter"` to `RAGAgentName` union - Add `rewriter` entry to `DEFAULT_RETRIEVAL_CONFIGS` with content types `USER_UPLOAD`, `PLAYBOOK`; `topK: 5`, `minScore: 0.12`
Task 4: Register in Agent Catalog Make the agent available to the resilient runner and discoverable in the agent registry. - Update `packages/mastra/src/agents/registry.ts`: add to `AGENT_CATALOG` array and `LAUNCH_AGENTS` object - Update `package ``` ### Step 3: Create Sub-Issues Split the work into logical sub-issues. Each sub-issue should: - Be implementable in under 2 hours - Focus on a single feature/change - Have a clear definition of done - Stay within the size constraints above For each sub-issue: 1. Create it with `gh issue create` 2. Add the `plan-me` label to the **child sub-issue** 3. Add "Part of #2577" to the body 4. Copy relevant sections from the original CodeRabbit plan Example sub-issue creation: ```bash # Create the sub-issue gh issue create --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes --title "feat: [Part 1] Implement X" --body "Part of #2577 ## Description ..." # Add label to the CHILD sub-issue (not the parent) gh issue edit --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes --add-label plan-me ``` ### Step 4: Update Parent Issue After creating all sub-issues: 1. Add a task list to the parent issue body listing each sub-issue 2. Remove the `plan-me` label from parent (if present) 3. Add the `decomposed` label to parent Example: ```bash # Update parent issue body with task list gh issue edit 2577 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes --body "... - [ ] Sub-issue #123: ... - [ ] Sub-issue #124: ..." # Update labels gh issue edit 2577 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes --remove-label plan-me --add-label decomposed ``` ### Step 5: Close Parent Issue After updating the parent issue: ```bash gh issue close 2577 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes ``` ### Step 6: Report Results After completing, summarize: - List of created sub-issues with their numbers - Confirmation that parent issue was updated and closed - Any files that were difficult to split
label: decompose-2577
